message:
feat(copy): add pane selector, output file, and enhanced filtering options

Extend the ntm copy command with direct pane targeting, file output,
and improved filtering capabilities for more flexible output capture workflows.

New Features (internal/cli/copy.go):

Pane Selector Syntax:
- Use session:pane to target specific panes directly
- Example: ntm copy myproject:1 copies from pane index 1
- Overrides agent type filters when specified
- paneMatchesSelector() handles index and ID matching

Output File Support:
- --output, -o flag to save to file instead of clipboard
- Bypasses clipboard entirely when file path provided
- Creates file with captured content
- Useful for automation and piping

Quiet Mode:
- --quiet flag suppresses confirmation output
- Cleaner output for scripting use cases
- Only errors are printed to stderr

Headers Control:
- --headers flag to include pane headers in output
- Defaults to off when --code is set (code blocks only)
- Allows explicit control over header inclusion

Clipboard Interface Refactoring:
- runCopy() now accepts clipboard.Clipboard interface parameter
- Enables dependency injection for testing
- Cleaner separation of concerns

Enhanced Result Tracking:
- New copyResult struct for JSON output
- Tracks Sources, Lines, Bytes, Destination
- Includes Pattern, Code, OutputPath metadata
- sourceLabels array for multi-pane attribution

Extract Command Update (internal/cli/extract.go):
- Use clipboard.New() and clipboard.Clipboard interface
- Explicit error handling for clipboard availability
- Report backend name in error messages

Test Infrastructure (internal/cli/copy_test.go):
- MockClipboard implementing clipboard.Clipboard interface
- Enables unit testing of clipboard interactions
- Interface compliance verification via type assertion

Example Usage:
  ntm copy myproject:1           # Copy from specific pane
  ntm copy myproject --all       # Copy all panes to clipboard
  ntm copy myproject --output out.txt  # Save to file
  ntm copy myproject --code --quiet    # Silent code extraction
  ntm copy myproject --pattern 'ERROR' # Filter by regex

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
